requirements.text: All kernel interfaces shall use fixed-width integer types; no interface shall depend on platform-defined type widths.

verified_by.case: interface types have the specified widths

requirements.text: Every kernel service shall return an explicit status code enumerating all failure modes of that service.

verified_by.case: status codes are distinct and explicit

requirements.text: The kernel shall perform no dynamic memory allocation; all storage shall be statically sized from configured limits (`aos_limits.h`).

verified_by.case: configured limits bound every kernel table

requirements.text: The kernel shall confine each partition's writes to its statically configured memory region (hardware-enforced on flight targets via `aos_hal_protect_select`).

verified_by.case: protection context selected per executed window

requirements.text: Each partition shall be in exactly one mode of {IDLE, COLD_START, WARM_START, NORMAL}; a partition shall be able to change only its own mode, and only via the legal transitions (COLD/WARM_START→NORMAL, NORMAL→IDLE); the health monitor may command any transition.

verified_by.case: first frame initializes partitions into NORMAL
idle partitions are not dispatched

requirements.text: Sampling ports shall deliver the most recent message with an explicit freshness indication derived from the configured refresh period; reads before any write shall be distinguishable from valid data.

verified_by.case: sampling read before any write -> EMPTY
sampling delivers latest value, fresh
sampling data goes stale after refresh period
oversize sampling write rejected

requirements.text: Queuing ports shall deliver messages in FIFO order from bounded storage; when full, new messages shall be rejected with an error and queued data shall never be overwritten.

verified_by.case: queuing is FIFO
full queue rejects send, preserves contents

requirements.text: The kernel shall reject any port operation by a partition that does not own the port, and any operation inconsistent with the port's configured kind or direction.

verified_by.case: partition cannot use another partition's port
direction enforced (read on a source port)
port lookup by name

requirements.text: The kernel shall schedule partitions per a fixed cyclic table of windows within a fixed major frame, configured before start and immutable at runtime; every window shall execute in order every frame.

verified_by.case: overlapping windows rejected
window beyond major frame rejected
subsequent frames step each partition once
frame time advances by exactly one major frame

requirements.text: The kernel shall detect any partition exceeding its window budget and any major-frame overrun, and shall report each to the health monitor (partition-level and module-level respectively).

verified_by.case: window overrun raises DEADLINE with configured action
frame overrun reported as module-level timing fault

requirements.text: The kernel shall access hardware exclusively through the HAL interface (`aos_hal.h`).

verified_by.case: kernel timing driven entirely by HAL clock

requirements.text: The HAL time base shall be monotonic non-decreasing for the duration of module operation.

verified_by.case: HAL time base is monotonic non-decreasing

requirements.text: Every partition window shall execute under an armed watchdog whose budget equals the window duration.

verified_by.case: watchdog armed for every executed window

requirements.text: The kernel shall apply the statically configured recovery action (LOG, IDLE, WARM_RESTART, COLD_RESTART, SAFE_STATE) for each (partition, error) pair; a partition shall not be able to raise errors against another partition.

verified_by.case: LOG action records without mode change
IDLE action idles the partition
WARM_RESTART action re-initializes partition
partition cannot raise against another partition
application step error routed to HM as APP error

requirements.text: On SAFE_STATE the kernel shall command the module safe state via the HAL; a module with an invalid or absent configuration shall never enter NORMAL operation.

verified_by.case: config fault triggers configured safe state
SAFE_STATE action enters module safe state

requirements.text: The kernel shall timestamp and record every health-monitor event in a bounded log that preserves the earliest events on saturation and counts losses; the log shall be readable only by the configured maintenance partition.

verified_by.case: log saturation preserves first faults
HM log restricted to maintenance partition

requirements.text: Before scheduling any partition, the kernel shall validate the entire module configuration (limits, name uniqueness, window ordering/fit, channel routing and message-size consistency, entry points, region definitions) and shall reject operation on any inconsistency.

verified_by.case: valid configuration accepted
window for unknown partition rejected
duplicate partition name rejected
duplicate port name rejected
non-mutual channel peers rejected
mismatched channel message size rejected
null entry point rejected

requirements.text: Cold start shall zeroize all partition regions and kernel state so module behaviour after restart is independent of prior history.

verified_by.case: cold restart zeroizes the partition region

requirements.text: Every module instance shall carry a non-empty configured identity (LRU and lane, e.g. "R100-FCC1-COM"); the kernel shall reject a configuration without one. (The same AOS executable is deployed to many LRUs; only the configuration differs.)

verified_by.case: module instance identity required and exposed

requirements.text: The IOM shall publish validated air data and inceptor commands each frame.

verified_by.case: IOM publishes fresh, fully valid air data

requirements.text: Inter-partition messages shall be fixed-size typed structures defined by the ICD (REV-ICD-001).

verified_by.case: ICD messages all fit configured port sizes

requirements.text: The IOM shall mark source data invalid whenever the underlying acquisition has failed.

verified_by.case: air-data fault clears validity bits at the source

requirements.text: In normal law the FCS shall command the elevator to track the commanded pitch rate (positive command → trailing-edge-up).

verified_by.case: nose-up command drives elevator trailing-edge up
zero error holds surface near neutral

requirements.text: The FCS shall limit elevator commands to ±25° under all input conditions.

verified_by.case: surface never exceeds authority limit

requirements.text: The FCS shall limit elevator rate of motion to 40°/s under all input conditions.

verified_by.case: surface motion respects slew limit

requirements.text: Above 12° angle of attack the FCS shall command nose-down proportional to the excursion, overriding nose-up demands, while preserving pilot nose-down authority.

verified_by.case: alpha protection overrides nose-up demand
protection inactive below onset

requirements.text: On loss or staleness of required air data the FCS shall revert to direct law within one frame, transient-free, and recover to normal law when valid data returns.

verified_by.case: invalid data reverts to direct law
air-data loss reverts FCS to direct law end-to-end
recovery returns to normal law

requirements.text: The FCS integrator shall be clamped (anti-windup) and shall not integrate against active alpha protection.

verified_by.case: integrator clamps under sustained error

requirements.text: On loss of the pilot command path the FCS shall command surfaces to neutral (failsafe) within one frame.

verified_by.case: FCS commands neutral surfaces when pilot path is lost

requirements.text: The DISP shall annunciate every flight-control law change (caution for DIRECT, warning for FAILSAFE, advisory for NORMAL recovery) and forward annunciations to the maintenance recorder.

verified_by.case: law changes annunciated and recorded by maintenance

requirements.text: The DISP shall annunciate stale flight-control data exactly once per staleness episode.

verified_by.case: stale flight-control data annunciated exactly once

requirements.text: The FMS shall publish a navigation solution (position, groundspeed, track, active waypoint) each frame.

verified_by.case: FMS publishes an advancing navigation solution

requirements.text: The MAINT partition shall collect EICAS messages and health-monitor events with bounded per-window processing, for Part 121 continuing-airworthiness reporting (§121.373).

verified_by.case: no health-monitor events in nominal operation

requirements.text: The R-100 baseline module shall host IOM, FCS, DISP, FMS, MAINT, and MON in a 50 ms major frame per the configured schedule, with all partitions reaching NORMAL mode within two frames of cold start.

verified_by.case: baseline configuration accepted and started
all partitions reach NORMAL within two frames
closed loop: doublet produces bounded, damped response

requirements.text: The MON partition shall check, every frame, that the COM surface command respects the certified envelope invariants: authority within ±25° (+monitor tolerance), slew within 40°/s (+monitor tolerance), and nose-down command whenever angle of attack exceeds the never-exceed value, using air data received on its own independently routed channel.

verified_by.case: closed loop runs with no nuisance trip
slew violation detected
missed alpha protection detected

requirements.text: The MON partition shall latch a trip after three consecutive violating frames, shall publish its status (trip, flags, violation count) every frame, and shall preserve a latched trip across warm restart (cold start clears it).

verified_by.case: authority violation detected and latched after 3 frames
latched trip persists after data returns to normal
warm restart preserves the trip; cold start clears it
transient violation (< 3 frames) does not trip

requirements.text: The MON partition shall flag a reversion inconsistency whenever the COM lane claims normal law while required air data is invalid, and shall flag loss of the COM output channel.

verified_by.case: normal law on invalid air data flagged
silent COM output channel flagged

requirements.text: The DISP partition shall annunciate a latched monitor trip as a warning exactly once per trip episode.

verified_by.case: trip annunciated as warning exactly once per episode

means_of_compliance.subject: Reusable software component
means_of_compliance.means: AC 20-148 RSC package: kernel + verification data certified once, reused per LRU and across the R-50/R-100 family

means_of_compliance.subject: Equipment, systems, network information security
means_of_compliance.means: Security risk assessment, protection, continuing airworthiness

means_of_compliance.subject: Continuing airworthiness reporting
means_of_compliance.means: §121.373 program fed by CMC/MAINT fault data
